page: Amazon Web Services c8g.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Neoverse-V2 ARM64 processor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides a 30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. This server does not include local storage or GPU hardware.

Benchmark data indicates exceptional multi-core and memory performance. The instance achieves top-tier rankings in memory bandwidth for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks, alongside a top-tier Passmark memory score. Multi-threaded operations perform exceptionally well, with top-tier results in Geekbench multi-core, software compilation with Clang, and Bzip3 decompression. Single-core performance is rated as average to strong, while database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput all reach top-tier benchmarks.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and high core density deliver strong multi-threaded performance and high throughput per resource. This makes the instance highly suitable for compute-intensive workloads such as software compilation, database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing. It also demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference text generation for both medium and large models.
description: Amazon Web Services c8g.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by 64 dedicated ARM64 physical cores using the AWS Neoverse-V2 processor at 2.8 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 128.0 GB of memory and a 30 Gbps baseline network interface, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate top-tier performance in memory bandwidth, multi-core processing, software compilation, and database operations. The instance delivers high resource density and efficiency for multi-threaded tasks. It is highly suited for compute-bound workloads, including software development pipelines, high-throughput web serving, Redis databases, and medium-to-large scale LLM inference.

tagline: A dedicated 64-core ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and exceptional multi-threaded performance for high-throughput workloads.

128.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core benchmark performance
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Optimized for software compilation, database operations, and web serving
